title: picea obovata var. alpestris
intro: Picea obovata var. alpestris, also known as the Mongolian spruce, is a subspecies of evergreen coniferous tree native to the mountainous regions of northern China, Russia, and Mongolia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, due to the presence of terpenoids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Picea obovata var. alpestris include reducing fever, treating respiratory issues, and alleviating skin irritations. The herbal preparations of Picea obovata var. alpestris include infusions, decoctions, and essential oils, which can be used to create topical creams and ointments, as well as teas for medicinal purposes.
